type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Adding zero-byte validation to resolve_image(), resolve_audio(), and resolve_document() methods in ImageBlock/AudioBlock/DocumentBlock classes - will raise ValueError if BytesIO buffer is empty

fields.note: Adding zero-byte validation to resolve_image(), resolve_audio(), and resolve_document() methods in ImageBlock/AudioBlock/DocumentBlock classes - will raise ValueError if BytesIO buffer is empty

content:
type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: ImageBlock.resolve_image()
code:     def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer
note: This is the final body of resolve_image() after my zero-byte validation change. Your resolve_image_with_size() insertion should go right after this exact text.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: ImageBlock.resolve_image + resolve_image_with_size
code:     def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        """
        buffer = resolve_binary(
            raw_bytes=self.image,
            path=self.path,
            url=str(self.url) if self.url else None,
            as_base64=as_base64,
        )
        buffer.seek(0, 2)
        size = buffer.tell()
        buffer.seek(0)
        if size == 0:
            raise ValueError("resolve_image returned zero bytes")
        return buffer

    def resolve_image_with_size(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> tuple[BytesIO, int]:
        """
        Resolve an image such that PIL can read it, and return its size in bytes.

        Args:
            as_base64 (bool): whether the resolved image should be returned as base64-encoded bytes

        Returns:
            tuple[BytesIO, int]: the resolved buffer and its size in bytes.

        """
        buffer = self.resolve_image(as_base64=as_base64)
        size = buffer.getbuffer().nbytes
        return buffer, size
note: My task spec explicitly requires the seek(0,2)/tell()/seek(0) pattern and the exact message 'resolve_<type> returned zero bytes' - please use this version instead of getbuffer().nbytes. Kept your resolve_image_with_size() as-is, just calling self.resolve_image().
